stories.title: Kevin Warsh Sworn In As New Fed Chair| Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 5/22/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Mike McKee and Stuart Paul discuss the future of the Federal Reserve under Kevin Warsh after he was sworn in as the new fed chair. Then, Qualcomm's head of Automotive, Robotics and IoT Businesses Nakul Duggal talks the semiconductor company's new deal with Stellantis to advance vehicle tech as the self-driving landscape grows across the automotive industry. Plus, President and CEO of S

stories.title: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair as Trump seeks interest rate cuts
stories.summary: Despite President Donald Trump's demands for lower interest rates, markets are betting the Fed will stay on hold through most, if not all, of 2026.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh Sworn In As Fed Chair | Real Yield 5/22/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: Societe Generale Americas Head of Research Subadra Rajappa, Franklin Templeton Fixed Income CIO Sonal Desai, Capital Group Fixed Income Asset Class Lead Margaret Steinbach, and Baird Funds Co-Chief Investment Officer Warren Pierson. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Rep. Amo Discusses DNC Autopsy, War Powers Vote
stories.summary: Gabe Amo, Democratic Representative of Rhode Island, joins Joe Mathieu on Bloomberg's Balance of Power to discuss the DNC's autopsy of the 2024 election as well as the likelihood of Congress passing a war powers resolution, saying the 'long-term solution' is to end the war in Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Zoom’s Anthropic Investment Has Netted the Company $1 Billion
stories.summary: Zoom Communications Inc., the videoconferencing company, has netted about $1 billion on an investment it made in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC in early 2023.

stories.title: Treasury yields are mixed as investors digest Fed chair swearing in, bond market volatility
stories.summary: The volatility in the bond market continued as new Fed chair Kevin Warsh was sworn in on Friday.

stories.title: Anthropic to Close Over $30 Billion Round as Soon as Next Week
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC is set to close its latest round of funding, which may top $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion, as soon as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, vaulting ahead of rival OpenAI to become the world’s most valuable AI startup.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh Sworn in as New Federal Reserve Chair
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the 17th chair of the federal reserve in a ceremony at the White House Friday. Speaking after taking his oath, Warsh called it the 'honor of a lifetime' to re-enter public service and promised that he will lead a 'reform-oriented' Fed. Despite fears around Fed independence, President Trump told Warsh to 'just do your own thing and do a great job.' (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Nasdaq Gets Approval From SEC to List Bitcoin Index Options
stories.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission has given the go-ahead for Nasdaq Inc. to list index options based on the price of Bitcoin, the latest sign that Wall Street is becoming more tightly integrated with the world of digital assets.

stories.title: What Europe Wants to Know About Trump, the War and the Fed
stories.summary: Next steps with Iran and the central bank’s independence were front and center.

stories.title: Warsh Sworn In As Fed Chair | Balance of Power: Early Edition 5/22/2026
stories.summary: On the early edition of Balance of Power, Bloomberg Washington Correspondent Joe Mathieu discusses Kevin Warsh's swearing-in as the next Chair of the Federal Reserve. On today's show, Kpler Head of Policy and Geopolitical Risk Michelle Brouhard, Harvard Kennedy School Ash Center Visiting Democracy Fellow Jeanne Sheehan Zaino, Friday Reporter Public Affairs Founder Lisa Camooso Miller, Goldman Sach

stories.title: Credit Entry Points Incredibly Attractive: Steinbach
stories.summary: Margaret Steinbach, fixed income director at Capital Group, and Warren Pierson, co-chief investment officer of the Baird Funds, join Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Junk debt is beating just about everything else in fixed-income markets after surging yields wiped out gains on most other bonds. Yet with high-yield credit spreads near two-decade lows, investor unease is building. (Source: Bloo

stories.title: Warsh Most Hawkish Fed Nominee in 20 Years: Desai
stories.summary: Sonal Desai, CIO of Franklin Templeton Fixed Income, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Kevin Warsh, who has promised the biggest shakeup in decades at the US central bank, was sworn into office Friday in a White House ceremony as the 17th chair of the Fed. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Micron CEO Sees Memory Chip Shortage Lasting Beyond 2026
stories.summary: Sanjay Mehrotra, President, Chairman and CEO of Micron joined Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall to discuss the company's significant expansion of DRAM manufacturing in the US. He also discussed the demand for AI, and how he sees memory chip shortage lasting beyond 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: UAE Joins Saudis, Qatar in Urging Trump Not to Restart War
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates has made a more concerted push for an end to the Iran war in recent days, joining Saudi Arabia and Qatar in urging US President Donald Trump to give negotiations a chance, according to several people familiar with the matter. Michelle Brouhard, Head of Policy and Geopolitical Risk at Kpler joins Balance of Power to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Iran War Saps US Military Ahead of Any Potential China Conflict
stories.summary: A top Pentagon official’s remarks that the US had halted arms sales to Taiwan to ensure sufficient munitions for the Iran war are the latest sign that the conflict has sapped American military readiness, including for a potential confrontation with China.

stories.title: SEC Delays Plan Allowing for Crypto Versions of US Stocks
stories.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission is delaying a plan to provide broad exemptions for US crypto firms to trade tokenized assets linked to stocks, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Trump wants new Fed chair to be 'totally independent'
stories.summary: The US president piled major pressure on Kevin Warsh's predecessor to cut interest rates.

stories.title: Goldman's Kaplan on Warsh as Fed Chair, Rates and AI
stories.summary: Robert Kaplan, Goldman Sachs vice chair and former president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas, expects Kevin Warsh to be an "excellent" Fed chair. stories.title: Gilt relief rally sends yields to biggest weekly drop since 2023
stories.summary: Burnham pledge to stick to fiscal rules and pullback from bets on higher BoE interest rates drive rebound

stories.title: Are Fintech Apps Already Dead?
stories.summary: Is it the end of fintech and investing apps as we know it? Atomic Invest CEO David Dindi joins Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about the future of finance and why it belongs to AI companions, not apps. He says investing apps could disappear within a decade as AI assistants manage portfolios for consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Surge in 'risk-free' treasury yields sends bond investors in search of better opportunities
stories.summary: Treasury yield surge shows bond market is not 'risk free' after all, but there's opportunity for fixed-income investors in intermediates, BBBs and high yield.

stories.title: Bond Traders Bet Fed Under Warsh Will Hike Rates This Year
stories.summary: Bond traders are fully pricing in an interest-rate hike by the Federal Reserve this year, a sign of conviction in the market that Chair Kevin Warsh will need to move quickly to combat inflation.

stories.title: The Tech Download: What you might have missed in Nvidia’s earnings — a $200 billion opportunity and edge computing

stories.title: U.S., Iran signal peace progress — but remain at odds over enriched uranium, Strait of Hormuz tolls

stories.title: Energy Leaders Gather for 'Operation Gigawatt Summit'

stories.title: Senegal’s Faye Sacks Sonko Government Amid Widening Policy Rift
stories.summary: Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye dissolved the government and dismissed Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, increasing the risk of political unrest after months of simmering tensions between the two leaders.

stories.title: SpaceX Starship Rocket Takes Off From Texas
stories.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX successfully launched its Starship rocket from Texas. It reached space a few minutes later and deployed 20 dummy Starlink satellites. The closely watched flight is a key demonstration of upgrades from previously launched Starship prototypes, including increasing the rocket’s power and capability. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Abrego Garcia Wins Dismissal of US Human-Smuggling Case
stories.summary: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed human-smuggling charges against Salvadoran migrant Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia after concluding the US government brought the case in retaliation for his legal fight against being deported.

stories.title: Disney's 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' tallies lowest Thursday preview sales in franchise history
stories.summary: Disney's "Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied $12 million in Thursday night previews, the lowest collection of advance tickets in franchise history.

stories.title: Governance Concerns Over SpaceX IPO & the Upside of Financing AI
stories.summary: New York City Comptroller Mark Levine discusses the governance concerns over the SpaceX IPO. He talks about the potential legislative changes that could be made to curb these mega-IPOs. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Two top Walmart executives leave company under new CEO John Furner
stories.summary: Tom Ward, chief operating officer of Walmart's warehouse chain Sam's Club, is retiring, and Cedric Clark, Walmart's EVP of U.S. store operations, is leaving.

stories.title: Equatorial Leans Against Bidding on Copasa Privatization
stories.summary: Equatorial SA is unlikely to bid on a key stake in Copasa, the Brazilian water utility that’s being privatized by the government of Minas Gerais state, according to people familiar to the matter.

stories.title: Fmr. Fed Governor Says Warsh, Powell Both Natural Leaders
stories.summary: Former Fed Governor Betsy Duke discusses the swearing in of new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. She says that both Kevin Warsh and former Fed Chair Jerome Powell are both natural leaders. She discusses the outlook of the Fed going forward with its new chairman. She speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Atomic Monster President on Business of Horror Films

stories.title: US Weighs Chip Tariffs to Spur Domestic Growth, Trade Chief Says

stories.title: Wind-Permit Stall Is Threatening $50 Billion in US Developments

stories.title: New York Tax on NYC Cash Home Purchases Close to Collapsing
